body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
biweekly updates

Also changes our debug plots to no longer produce plots for spatially constant ClimaCore fields. This is done to prevent errors in generating the heatmaps, e.g. as was seen in [this build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/6785#0197c7ac-19c2-4ea1-be5d-1cea80cba51e). The axis is still constructed with the extrema in the header, so we can still see the value of the field (this contains the same information as plotting a constant field in the figure).

I wasn't able to identify which package update caused the plotting error, and I also got this error when running my reproducer on main, and it came up in this past weekend's [longruns](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-longruns/builds/908#0197bab4-5d03-41c2-b6f6-fdae14c08729/170-721). So I figured the best workaround is to stop plotting constant plots. I opened an issue to keep track of this behavior here https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/issues/1422

Closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/issues/1423

## Package updates
```
Updating `~/clima/ClimaCoupler.jl/experiments/ClimaEarth/Project.toml`
```

Will merge after https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/pull/1416 for plotting fix
title: update dependencies
